item.title: BGB, locals foil two more BSF push-in bids
item.description: Members of the Border Guard Bangladesh and local people foiled two more push-in attempts of the Indian Border Security Force at two border points in Lalmonirhat and Meherpur districts in the early hours of Friday....

item.title: Controversies deepen over Shahe Alam’s alleged misdeeds
item.description: Local government state minister Mir Shahe Alam continues to stay at the centre of controversies with the arrest of a newspaper’s acting editor and the revelation that a school in Bogura’s Shibganj was being renamed after him....

item.title: MBPLC gets BB award for employment generation
item.description: Mercantile Bank PLC has recently been awarded a certificate by the Bangladesh Bank for its significant contribution to inclusive finance and enterprise development....

item.title: IFIC Bank signs deal with Best Western Plus Bay Hills
item.description: IFIC Bank PLC and Best Western Plus Bay Hills, part of the international hotel chain, signed a memorandum of understanding on Thursday....

item.title: Samsung rolls out offers at K-Goods Festa
item.description: Two-day ‘K-Goods Festa 2026’ started on Friday at East Court of Jamuna Future Park in the capital, said a press release....

item.title: Heidelberg Materials approves 11pc cash dividend
item.description: Heidelberg Materials Bangladesh PLC on Thursday approved 11 per cent cash dividend, said a press release....

item.title: Walton begins exporting PCBA to US
item.description: Walton began exporting printed circuit board assemblies or PCBA and various technology products to the United States on Thursday, said a press release....

item.title: BB eases ship lease remittance rules
item.description: The Bangladesh Bank has allowed domestic shipping companies to remit vessel lease rentals abroad, in a move aimed at strengthening and modernising the country’s maritime financing framework....

item.title: Haribhanga harvest turns Rangpur into trade hub
item.description: Every summer the arrival of Haribhanga mangoes brings a wave of excitement to northern Bangladesh. But in Rangpur and surrounding districts, the famed sweet and juicy fruit represents far more than a seasonal treat....

item.title: EU to develop new tools to tackle China export flood
item.description: EU leaders agreed during talks Thursday that the bloc must develop beefed-up trade defences to curb a surge of Chinese exports deemed an existential threat to European industry by Brussels — while simultaneously seeking ‘constructive dialogue’ with Beijing....

item.title: European robotics start-ups go up against Chinese heavyweights
item.description: Humanoid robots able to perform tasks from grape harvesting to welcoming visitors were front and centre at France’s Vivatech trade fair this week, with European firms looking to fill niches beyond what dominant Chinese giants can offer....

item.title: Cuba adopts historic package of free-market reforms
item.description: Cuban lawmakers Thursday adopted nearly 200 historic free-market reforms aimed at rescuing the communist island from a severe crisis aggravated by a US oil blockade....

item.title: After soaring debut, SpaceX begins to return to earth
item.description: After a record IPO followed by a sky-bound trio of sessions, shares of SpaceX started to come back down to earth on Thursday....

item.title: Mukesh Ambani’s Jio Platforms to file for IPO
item.description: One of India’s top telecommunications and AI companies will file papers for an IPO on Friday, billionaire Mukesh Ambani said, kicking off the process for one of the country’s most-awaited stock market debuts....

item.title: Japan rice prices fall first time in 3.5 years
item.description: Rice prices in Japan finally fell in May for the first time in three and half years, a government official said Friday, following sharp rises for the staple that put the government under huge pressure....

item.title: US opens trade probe against Germany over pharma pricing
item.description: The United States has started an investigation over pharmaceutical pricing policies and other practices in Germany, a move that could lead to fresh tariffs....

item.title: 3-day tourism festival begins at Srimangal
item.description: A three-day Harmony Festival showcasing the rich cultural heritage of ethnic communities began at Srimangal on Friday, aiming to promote sustainable cultural tourism, community-based tourism and local economic development....

item.title: Agrani Bank holds business review meeting
item.description: Agrani Bank PLC organised a business review meeting for 21 corporate branches on Thursday at the boardroom of its head office in the capital, said a press release....

item.title: WEAB seeks govt support for women-led businesses
item.description: Women Entrepreneurs Association of Bangladesh on Thursday described the proposed national budget for fiscal year 2026-27 as timely, realistic and inclusive, saying it has been designed to address current economic challenges and ensure sustainable development....

item.title: National Tea Day to be celebrated today
item.description: Bangladesh Tea Board will celebrate National Tea Day 2026 at Srimangal of Moulvibazar district today, on June 20, with the theme ‘Development of the Tea Industry, Greening the Economy.’...

item.title: Uniform 5pc VAT for restaurants, caterers sought
item.description: The Bangladesh Restaurant Owners Association on Friday urged the government to introduce a uniform value added tax rate for restaurants and catering services and withdraw the proposed source tax on essential commodities in the national budget for the 2026-27 financial year....

item.title: Gold price drops by Tk 4,374 per bhari
item.description: The Bangladesh Jewellers Association on Friday reduced the price of 22-carat gold by Tk 4,374 a bhari, setting the new rate at Tk 228,556....

item.title: Mirroring mind and selfishness
item.description: BEFORE Justus von Liebig, a German chemist, invented modern mirror by plastering a thin layer of silver onto glass, most people had ended their lives without even knowing what they had looked like. Some only occasionally happened to look at fragmented and......

item.title: Can budget deliver on promise of equality?
item.description: THE national budget is more than a financial statement. It reflects a country’s development priorities, economic plans and political commitments. Every year, when the budget is announced, the government makes various promises regarding development, employment, social protection......

item.title: AI in vaccine making
item.description: VACCINES have saved millions of lives, from smallpox to polio to Covid-19. But making them the traditional way is slow, expensive and often involves a great deal of trial and error. It can easily take a decade from the first laboratory experiment to a vaccine finally reaching someone’s arm....

item.title: Making citizens partners in policy execution
item.description: THE key element in democracy is to ensure the views and aspirations of the people are adequately reflected in policies undertaken by the state. This will ensure due reflection of people’s will and preferences in matters of national interests. Accordingly, governments in several democracies......

item.title: Mending road regime that broke long ago
item.description: THE government has yet to attend to many issues to restore discipline to the road transport regime. The photograph that New Age published on June 19 is partially illustrative of how bad the road regime is. The photograph shows a bus, rundown and ramshackle, at the busy......

item.title: Unresolved Sagar-Runi case speaks of enduring impunity
item.description: A DHAKA court’s decision on June 18 to once again extend the deadline for the submission of police investigation report on the murder of journalist couple Sagar Sarwar and Meherun Runi has deepened the disappointment of the families, further eroding their......

item.title: 60,588 Hajj pilgrims return home
item.description: As of Thursday, 60,588 Bangladeshi pilgrims returned home from Saudi Arabia after performing this year’s Hajj, which took place on May 26....

item.title: Woman says husband divorced her over twin girls
item.description: A woman in Jhenaidah has alleged that her husband divorced her after she gave birth to twin daughters, highlighting the continued discrimination and abuse faced by women for bearing girl children in Bangladesh....

item.title: Sultanganj river port set to reopen after 2 years
item.description: After remaining closed for over two years, Sultanganj River Port in Rajshahi’s Godagari upazila is finally set to reopen....

item.title: ‘Teesta Mega Project’ to be implemented with domestic funding: minister
item.description: Water resources minister Shahid Uddin Chowdhury Annie said that prime minister Tarique Rahman has initiated a mega project centred around the Teesta River and this ‘Teesta Mega Project’ will be executed at the earliest, with fully domestic funding and a mix of domestic and foreign technical expertise....

item.title: Jamaat chief accuses govt of one-party push
item.description: Leader of the opposition and Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami amir Shafiqur Rahman on Friday alleged that the government was trying to establish a one-party rule in the country by appointing its ‘own supporters’ to key positions in banks, universities and local government institutions....

item.title: KRTC holds seminar on practice gap in science education
item.description: The Kekuleon Research and Training Centre held a seminar titled Bridging the Theory: Practice Gap in Science Education in Bangladesh at the Bangla Academy in Dhaka on Wednesday....

item.title: Brahmaputra erosion leaves 30 families homeless in Kurigram
item.description: The Brahmaputra River has devoured the homesteads of at least 30 families in Goalpuri Char under Jatrapur union of Sadar upazila in Kurigram over one week, with over 100 other families remaining under threat of erosion....

item.title: Comilla University JCD gets new committee
item.description: Bangladesh Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal, the student wing of ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party, has announced a new 12-member convening committee for its Comilla University unit after a gap of five years....

item.title: Man held over rape of mentally-challenged woman in Naogaon
item.description: Members of the Rapid Action Battalion arrested a man on charges of raping a woman with mental disability in Naogaon....

item.title: Experts demand strict enforcement of environmental law
item.description: The Pran Prokriti Poribesh Protibesh Rakkha Jatio Committee demanded strict enforcement of existing environmental law to protect Dhaka’s disappearing greeneries....

item.title: CMP extends deadline for inquiry into cricketer Nayeem harassment
item.description: The investigation committee formed to probe allegations of police harassment of national cricketer Nayeem Hasan has been given four additional working days to submit its report....

item.title: DPE warns teachers on social media rules
item.description: The Directorate of Primary Education has warned government primary school teachers against using social media in ways that violate government regulations and harm the image of the government, public institutions and the primary education system....

item.title: Journalist Azahar shown arrested in anti-terrorism case
item.description: Senior journalist Azahar Ali Sarker was shown arrested in an anti-terrorism case on Thursday, a month after securing interim bail from the High Court....

item.title: DMP bans carrying sharp objects in Tazia march
item.description: The Dhaka Metropolitan Police have banned all kinds of arms, including sharp weapons, during the Tazia procession brought out on the occasion of Ashura, which will be observed on June 26 this year. ...

item.title: Rain likely nationwide for 5 days: BMD
item.description: Rainfall is likely to continue across the country over the next five days as the monsoon remains fairly active over Bangladesh, according to the latest weather forecast issued by the Bangladesh Meteorological Department....

item.title: PM urges investment in education, health
item.description: Emphasising that investment in children’s education and health is the most important investment in nation-building, prime minister Tarique Rahman has issued a message on the occasion of the ‘Primary School Gold Cup Football Tournament (Boys and Girls) 2026,’ which is set to begin on Saturday....

item.title: Russian strikes on Ukraine kill three
item.description: Russia renewed its strikes on Ukraine Friday, killing three people including an eight-year-old girl, Ukrainian officials said....

item.title: 50 govt job seekers arrested for exam cheating in Dhaka
item.description: Fifty candidates were arrested for cheating in the written examination on Friday for the post of assistant sub-food inspector of the Directorate General of Food in Dhaka, by using electronic devices in their ears....

item.title: Russia signals slower rate cuts amid high Ukraine war spending
item.description: Russia’s central bank on Friday cut its benchmark interest rate to 14.25 per cent from 14.5 per cent, less than analysts expected, and signalled a more cautious approach to further monetary policy easing as the Ukraine war takes a growing toll on Moscow’s finances....

item.title: Oil prices, shares steady after US-Iran talks postponed
item.description: Oil prices and stock markets stabilised Friday after postponement of Mideast peace deal talks and following big movements for markets this week on hopes of an end to the US-Iran conflict....

item.title: China to impose 55pc levy on Australian beef after quota hit
item.description: China said on Friday it will impose a tariff of 55 per cent on beef imports from Australia after shipments from the country hit Beijing’s annual limit....

item.title: In Denmark, doing away with bosses to boost performance
item.description: Danish company Clever is trying something it believes is clever indeed: it has done away with bosses to foster creativity and boost group performance....

item.title: India eyes biofertilisers after ME war stoked supply fears
item.description: Under a shed in north India, women scoop cow dung, lumps of unrefined sugar and flour to produce biofertiliser — part of a growing effort to tackle anxieties over the supply of chemical fertilisers....

item.title: USA beat Australia reach World Cup knockouts
item.description:
The United States advanced to the World Cup knockout rounds with a game to spare following an impressive 2-0 win over Australia Friday, stoking the hopes of an already hyped host nation.
...

item.title: Israel, Hezbollah agree ceasefire as US-Iran deal under strain
item.description: Israel and Hezbollah agreed a ceasefire on Friday, a US official said, after deadly exchanges between the two sides in Lebanon once again put a deal to end the war on Iran under strain......

item.title: Coast Guard rescues four fishermen at Kutubdia
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard rescued four fishermen after their fishing boat, which had been floating in the deep sea west of Kutubdia for two days after its engine failed, in Cox’s Bazar on Friday....

item.title: BRACU underwater vehicle wins best award at competition
item.description: Dubomini, an autonomous underwater vehicle built by BRAC University’s student robotics team BRACU DUBURI, has won the Best Project Award at the Project Showcasing Competition 2026 at the headquarters of Institution of Engineers, Bangladesh at Ramna in Dhaka recently....

item.title: Effective collab between univs, industries to be built: UGC member
item.description: Member of the University Grants Commission of Bangladesh Professor Abdullah-AL-Mamun at a discussion in Pabna has said that initiatives will be taken to build effective cooperation between universities and the industry....

item.title: Sukumar Roy scholarship launched at NU
item.description: A scholarship named Sukumar Roy Merit Scholarship was launched for students of the Bangla department at the National University in Gazipur on Thursday....

item.title: Labour rights orgs reject budget, demand jobs
item.description: Speakers at a seminar on Friday criticised the proposed national budget for the 2026–27 financial year, saying that it fails to address the needs of workers and does not reflect their expectations amid rising living costs and unemployment pressures....

item.title: Kane hails World Cup 'Wonderwall' singalong as England highlight
item.description: Harry Kane says England's emotional ‘Wonderwall’ singalong with fans after their opening World Cup win against Croatia was one of his top moments in the national shirt....

item.title: Mustafa Monwar’s condition slightly improves
item.description: Eminent artist Mustafa Monwar’s condition showed slight improvement on Friday as he continues to undergo treatment at the intensive care unit of a hospital in the capital....

item.title: Natun Natoker Utshab at BSA ends
item.description: The eight-day theatre festival Natun Natoker Utshab came to a close on Friday at the Experimental Theatre Hall of the Bangladesh Shilpakala Academy in the capital....

item.title: Sufia Kamal’s 115th birth anniv today
item.description: Today is the 115th anniversary of birth of the founding president of Bangladesh Mahila Parishad and a pioneer of Bengali women’s emancipation, poet Begum Sufia Kamal....

item.title: 1,537 minority families receive eviction threat since March: Hindu Mohajote
item.description: A platform of religious minority people on Friday alleged that 1,537 minority families across the country have been threatened with eviction since March this year....

item.title: Algeria file complaint over referee's decision on Messi tackle
item.description: Algeria have lodged a complaint with FIFA over ‘refereeing injustice’ in their opening World Cup loss to Argentina, particularly a tackle by Lionel Messi, an Algerian football federation source told AFP on Friday....

item.title: UGC Officers’ Association gets new body
item.description: The University Grants Commission of Bangladesh Officers’ Association on Thursday got new executive committee for the next two years with Md Mohibul Ahsan as president and Md Golam Dastagir as general secretary....

item.title: Healthy social culture key to civilised society: info minister
item.description: Information and broadcasting minister Zahir Uddin Swapon on Friday stressed the need for strengthening social and political culture, saying healthy interaction among people and vibrant social institutions are essential for building a civilised society....

item.title: Neymar? He is working remotely, jokes Lula
item.description:
Brazilian star Neymar is ‘the first player in the world to be called up to the national side and be working remotely’, Brazil President Luiz Inacio Lula de Silva joked on Friday.
...

item.title: BNP leader severely injured in stabbing at Mohammadpur
item.description: Unidentified assailants on Friday afternoon stabbed a Bangladesh Nationalist Party leader in front of his house in the capital’s Mohammadpur area, leaving him seriously injured....

item.title: Poet Al Mujahidi dies
item.description: Ekushey Padak-winning poet, veteran freedom fighter and long-time literary editor of the Daily Ittefaq, Al Mujahidi, died on Friday....

item.title: Police uniform colours change again
item.description: The government has changed the colour scheme of the police uniform again following the amendment of the police dress code....

item.title: Litton ruled out of last T20I against Australia
item.description:
Bangladesh captain Litton Das was ruled out from the ongoing T20I series against Australia, confirmed the Bangladesh Cricket Board on Friday.
...

item.title: LALMONIRHAT RAPE, MURDER: 10 sent to jail over vandalising vehicles, attacking police
item.description: A Lalmonirhat court on Friday sent 10 people to jail in connection with the two cases filed over obstructing government duties, vandalising vehicles, and attacking the police following a clash over the rape and murder of a seven-year-old girl....

item.description: 1:00 AM Live FIFA World Cup 2026: USA v Australia (Saturday) 4:00 AM Live FIFA World Cup 2026: Scotland v Morocco (Saturday) ...

item.title: 5 specialised children’s hospitals to open in 5 divisions
item.description: The government is set to launch five 200-bed specialised children’s hospitals in Khulna, Barishal, Rangpur, Rajshahi divisions and Cumilla district within the next six months, significantly expanding paediatric healthcare services across the country....

item.title: Youth lynched on theft suspicion in Rajshahi
item.description: A young man was allegedly lynched by a group of students on suspicion of theft in Rajshahi city on Thursday night....
